activities_projects.title: Quantitative Vital Capacity Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Measure and compare the lung capacity of individuals
materials: Large plastic jar (5L) with volume markings
steps: Fill the jar with water and invert it into the water tub without letting air in
Take a deep breath and exhale completely into the jar through the tube
Measure the volume of air that displaced the water (Vital Capacity)
Compare data among different age groups and analyze factors affecting capacity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the efficiency of gas exchange based on lung volume
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in measurement and logical analysis of volume variations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Enzymatic Breakdown Kinetics
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the effect of temperature on salivary amylase activity
materials: Ice, Room temperature water, Hot water (60°C)
steps: Prepare three test tubes with starch and saliva mixture
Place them in ice, room temperature, and hot water respectively
Perform an iodine test every 2 minutes for each tube
Record the time taken for the blue color to stop appearing (indicating complete digestion)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that life processes are temperature-dependent chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed data table and conclusion on optimum enzyme temperature (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simulating Double Circulation
activities_projects.objective: Model the independent pathways of pulmonary and systemic circulation
materials: Two different colored liquids (e.g., blue and red dyed water)
steps: Construct a circuit where 'blood' must pass through the heart twice to complete one cycle
Demonstrate the separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood
Simulate a 'septal defect' (hole in the heart) and observe the mixing of colors
Explain the efficiency of 4-chambered hearts vs 3-chambered hearts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the evolutionary advantage of double circulation in mammals
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the model flow and explanation of septal function (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Microscopic Examination of Blood Smears
activities_projects.objective: Identify different types of blood cells and their morphologies
steps: Focus the slide under low and then high power magnification
Identify and sketch Biconcave RBCs and varied shapes of WBCs
Count the approximate ratio of RBCs to WBCs in one field of view
Research why RBCs lack a nucleus in mature mammals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates cellular structure to physiological function (oxygen transport vs. immunity)
activities_projects.evaluation: Detail of sketches and accuracy in identifying cell types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Osmoregulation and Kidney Load
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the effect of salt intake on urine concentration (Theoretical)
steps: Analyze a dataset showing water intake vs. urine output volume
Calculate the 'Concentration Factor' of urea in different scenarios
Plot the relationship between salt consumption and the workload on nephrons
Explain the role of ADH (Antidiuretic Hormone) in this process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the homeostatic role of kidneys in maintaining fluid balance
activities_projects.evaluation: Mathematical accuracy and depth of homeostatic reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigating Capillary Exchange
activities_projects.objective: Study the rate of diffusion across semi-permeable membranes
steps: Fill dialysis tubing with a starch-glucose mixture and place in a beaker of water
Test the beaker water for glucose and starch after 30 minutes
Explain why glucose passed through but starch did not
Relate this to nutrient exchange in human capillaries
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the molecular basis of nutrient transport in tissues
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct interpretation of diffusion based on molecular size (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Haemoglobin-Oxygen Dissociation Research
activities_projects.objective: Explore the chemical affinity of haemoglobin for various gases
steps: Research why Carbon Monoxide ($CO$) is more dangerous than $CO_2$
Analyze the 'Oxygen Dissociation Curve' and how pH affects it (Bohr Effect)
Explain why high-altitude dwellers have higher RBC counts
Draft a report on 'Chemical Adaptations for Respiration'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates chemistry and biology to understand gas transport at molecular levels
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific depth of the report and understanding of gas affinity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anatomy of Cardiac Rhythm
activities_projects.objective: Understand the electrical conduction system of the heart
steps: Trace the electrical path from the SA Node to the AV Node and Purkinje fibers
Explain the delay at the AV node and why it is essential for ventricular filling
Research the function of an artificial pacemaker
Diagram the sequence of a single cardiac cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the coordination between electrical signals and muscular contraction
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the conduction pathway diagram and timing logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anaerobic Threshold in Muscles
activities_projects.objective: Investigate lactic acid buildup during strenuous activity
steps: Perform rapid repetitions of the hand exerciser until muscle fatigue occurs
Record the time and number of reps to reach 'failure'
Explain the chemical transition from aerobic to anaerobic respiration in muscle cells
Research the fate of lactic acid in the liver (Cori Cycle)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands metabolic shifts and the concept of 'Oxygen Debt'
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of muscle fatigue and recovery (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Evolution of Excretory Products
activities_projects.objective: Compare Ammonia, Urea, and Uric Acid across animal groups
steps: Categorize animals as Ammonotelic, Ureotelic, or Uricotelic
Correlate the type of waste produced with the availability of water in the animal's habitat
Explain why birds produce uric acid instead of liquid urine
Calculate the energy cost vs. water-saving benefits for each product
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies ecological principles to understand physiological adaptations
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical correlation between habitat and excretory chemistry (10 marks)
